name: Cloudflare
description: Content delivery, security, and edge platform providing CDN, DNS, Workers, and many networking services accessible via APIs.

auth_methods.note: Cloudflare’s Authentication documentation states the API uses API tokens (recommended; Authorization: Bearer), the Global API Key (X-Auth-Email and X-Auth-Key headers), and OAuth 2.0.

credential_access.note: Cloudflare’s API Tokens documentation describes creating and managing API tokens in the Cloudflare dashboard (Manage Account > API Tokens) and notes the Global API Key is available in the dashboard. The page confirms self-serve access via dashboard, but does not explicitly state free-plan eligibility.

api_surface.note: Cloudflare exposes a broad REST API surface (https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/) covering zones, DNS, account settings, Workers, and more; extensive developer docs exist for many product areas.

buildability.note: Cloudflare provides public REST APIs and supports self-serve authentication methods (API tokens, Global API Key, and OAuth 2.0). API tokens can be created in the Cloudflare dashboard, so an integration can be built and tested today without contacting sales for core functionality.

queries: Cloudflare API authentication API Tokens Global API Key OAuth
Cloudflare API REST endpoints api.cloudflare.com client v4
Cloudflare signup free plan API access
Cloudflare enterprise contact sales enterprise-only APIs
